preamble: The shift from the voiceless velar fricative [x] to the voiceless labiodental fricative [f] is a form of labialization. It typically occurs when the velar sound is preceded by a rounded vowel or a labial glide, causing the point of articulation to migrate from the velum to the lips.

examples.note: The final velar fricative labialized after the rounded vowel.
German regularly shifted this fricative to a labial in specific lexemes.

sources: Baugh & Cable: A History of the English Language
